claim_level: cross-core .holo traversal witness attempt on real hardware

conditions.WORMHOLE=1: full protocol: opening coupling (writer holds family LLC-resident) + observer unscramble (inverse-permuted de-permuted probe)

matrix_real_accuracy.note: per core-pair, per condition, across 6 independent seed windows (sw 0-5)

tuning_sweep_pair_0_1.stronger_coupling_hurts: OPEN_REFRESH 128/512/2048 collapse back toward chance (~0.25-0.31); sustained co-access homogenizes shared L3 residency

phase_relational_readout.method: correlate de-permuted family residency with exact encode ramp for true theta vs shuffled-theta null
phase_relational_readout.interpretation: true-theta correlation indistinguishable from null; relational/phase structure does not survive the cross-core channel

conclusion: On real silicon the full wormhole protocol (opening coupling + observer unscramble) produces a small, reproducible lift over the naive read (ON 0.311 vs OFF 0.251 mean across 3 pairs x 6 seeds) so the mechanism is directionally real, but it does NOT clear the matched-null gates anywhere (best real accuracy 0.40 << 0.60 gate; real-vs-pseudo floor <= 0.544 << 0.95 gate) and is robust to coupling/carrier tuning. The naive read reproduces chance (~0.25, matching Codex's 0.275). The relational/phase tag does not survive. The sim-predicted ~0.79 does not hold: the sim modeled the cross-core channel as a clean key-permutation the observer perfectly inverts, but the physical cache-residency signal on this shared-L3 Phenom is too noisy/weak for the de-permute to recover the .holo footprint above the discriminating nulls.
what_holds_on_hardware: catalytic byte-tape restore is exact (3840/3840 every run)
naive cross-core read reproduces chance, matching Codex's 0.275
opening coupling + unscramble gives a small reproducible lift over naive
what_does_not_hold: cross-core mode recovery above matched nulls (the witness)
